title: An observationally based energy balance for the Earth since 1950
justification: The German contribution to Estimating the Circulation and Climate of the Ocean (GECCO) ocean assimilation model suggests that since the mid-1990s more heat may have been going into the deep ocean

title: Analysis of large-scale ocean bottom pressure variability in the North Pacific
justification: We also use monthly average maps of OBP from a version of the MIT general circulation model [ Marshall et al., 1997] that is run at Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) as part of the Estimating the Circulation and Climate of the Ocean (ECCO) consortium, which we will denote as JPL_ECCO.

title: Response of the global ocean to Greenland and Antarctic ice melting
justification: The set-up of the control run is essentially similar to the one used for the global 1/C176 global synthesis of ocean observations performed by the ECCO (Estimating the Circulation and Climate of the Ocean) consortium.
